name: patternguard
description: Guard AI-agent code changes against pattern drift: plan the blast radius, execute within bounds, and review sibling consistency before committing.

interface.shortDescription: Guard agent code changes against pattern drift
interface.longDescription:
Three coordinated skills for codebases operated by AI agents and subagents.

patternguard-planner — Maps every file a change touches before writing a single line. Classifies logic (route-specific, cross-cutting, utility), records duplicated patterns in the index, marks template candidates when repetition justifies it, and promotes them to template mode only when a generator command already exists. Ensures .patternguard/ exists, outputs .patternguard/CODEBASE_INDEX.yaml, .patternguard/changes/index.yaml, focused change-impact files, and a structured handoff note. Works on greenfield and existing projects.

patternguard-executor — Executes code changes with full scope awareness. Self-selects between four modes: Bootstrap (existing project, no index), Solo (simple task, no subagents), Orchestrator (large scope, spawns subagents with task envelopes), Subagent (received an envelope, executes one file). Candidate-only patterns stay duplicated; template-backed patterns regenerate only when template and generator.command are present. Only the orchestrator writes to PatternGuard index files.

patternguard-reviewer — Reviews changes after execution. Verifies correctness, completeness, and sibling consistency. Never edits code — only reads and produces verdicts: approved, changes-needed, or escalate. Self-selects between Solo, Orchestrator, and Subagent modes.
defaultPrompt: Use patternguard-planner to map the structure for a new payment validation feature
Use patternguard-executor to apply the fraud threshold change across all routes
Use patternguard-reviewer to verify the executor's changes before committing
